text: {"claims":[{"statement":"Bird and reptile eggs consist of a protective eggshell, albumen (egg white), and vitellus (egg yolk), contained within various thin membranes.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"Bird and reptile eggs consist of a protective [eggshell](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eggshell \"Eggshell\"), albumen ( [egg white](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Egg_white \"Egg white\")), and vitellus ( [egg yolk](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yolk \"Yolk\")), contained within various [thin membranes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eggshell_membrane \"Eggshell membrane\").","entailment":"The quoted sentence directly lists the components of bird and reptile eggs, establishing the claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"The egg yolk is suspended in the egg white by one or two spiral bands of tissue called the chalazae.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"The egg yolk is suspended in the egg white by one or two spiral bands of tissue called the [chalazae](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chalaza \"Chalaza\") (from the Greek word [χάλαζα](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%CF%87%CE%AC%CE%BB%CE%B1%CE%B6%CE%B1 \"wikt:χάλαζα\"), meaning 'hailstone' or 'hard lump').","entailment":"The sentence explicitly describes the chalazae as the structures that suspend the yolk in the white, supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"The shape of a chicken egg resembles a prolate spheroid with one end larger than the other and has cylindrical symmetry along the long axis.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"The shape of a chicken egg resembles a [prolate spheroid](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prolate_spheroid \"Prolate spheroid\") with one end larger than the other and has cylindrical symmetry along the long axis.","entailment":"The quoted description of the egg’s geometry directly asserts the claimed shape characteristics.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"The larger end of the egg contains an air cell that forms when the contents of the egg cool down and contract after it is laid.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"The larger end of the egg contains an air cell that forms when the contents of the egg cool down and contract after it is laid.","entailment":"The sentence explains the origin and location of the air cell, matching the claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"A very fresh egg has a small air cell and receives a grade of AA.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"A very fresh egg has a small air cell and receives a grade of AA.","entailment":"The quoted observation links freshness, air‑cell size, and grading, providing empirical support for the claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"As the size of the air cell increases and the quality of the egg decreases, the grade moves from AA to A to B.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"As the size of the air cell increases and the quality of the egg decreases, the grade moves from AA to A to B.","entailment":"The sentence describes a correlation between air‑cell size, egg quality, and grading, supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"A very old egg will float in the water and should not be eaten, especially if a foul odor can be detected if the egg is cracked open.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"A very old egg will float in the water and should not be eaten,[\\[20\\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eggs_as_food#cite_note-McGee-20) especially if a foul odor can be detected if the egg is cracked open.[\\[21\\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eggs_as_food#cite_note-21)","entailment":"The quoted passage reports observed behavior of old eggs and a safety recommendation, providing empirical evidence for the claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Eggshell color is caused by pigment deposition during egg formation in the oviduct and may vary according to species and breed.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"Eggshell color is caused by [pigment](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pigment \"Pigment\") deposition during egg formation in the [oviduct](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oviduct \"Oviduct\") and may vary according to [species](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Species \"Species\") and [breed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Breed \"Breed\"), from the more common white or brown to pink or speckled blue-green.","entailment":"The sentence directly states the cause of shell color and its variability, matching the claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"The brown pigment is protoporphyrin IX, a precursor of heme, and the blue pigment is biliverdin, a product of the breakdown of heme.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"The brown pigment is [protoporphyrin IX](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Protoporphyrin_IX \"Protoporphyrin IX\"), a precursor of [heme](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heme \"Heme\"), and the blue pigment is [biliverdin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biliverdin \"Biliverdin\"), a product of the breakdown of heme.[\\[22\\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eggs_as_food#cite_note-Sachar-22)[\\[23\\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eggs_as_food#cite_note-23)","entailment":"The quoted description identifies the chemical nature of the brown and blue pigments, directly supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"Chicken breeds with white ear lobes lay white eggs, whereas chickens with red ear lobes lay brown eggs.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Generally, [chicken breeds](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_chicken_breeds \"List of chicken breeds\") with white ear lobes lay white eggs, whereas chickens with red ear lobes lay brown eggs.[\\[24\\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eggs_as_food#cite_note-24)","entailment":"The sentence reports an observed association between ear‑lobe color and egg shell color, providing empirical support.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"There is no significant link between shell color and nutritional value.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Although there is no significant link between shell color and nutritional value, there is often a cultural preference for one color over another (see [§Color of eggshell](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eggs_as_food#Color_of_eggshell) below).","entailment":"The quoted clause explicitly states the lack of a significant relationship, supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Eggshell membrane is a clear film lining the eggshell, visible when one peels a boiled egg.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"The [eggshell membrane](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eggshell_membrane \"Eggshell membrane\") is a clear film lining the eggshell, visible when one peels a boiled egg.","entailment":"The sentence directly defines the membrane’s appearance and location, matching the claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"These membranes may be used commercially as a dietary supplement.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"These membranes may be used commercially as a dietary supplement.[\\[26\\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eggs_as_food#cite_note-26)","entailment":"The quoted statement reports a commercial use of the membranes, providing empirical evidence for the claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"White is the common name for the clear liquid (also called the albumen or the glair/glaire) contained within an egg.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"\"White\" is the common name for the clear liquid (also called the albumen or the glair/glaire) contained within an egg.","entailment":"The sentence defines the term \"White\" as the clear liquid inside an egg, directly supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"In chickens, egg white is formed from the layers of secretions of the anterior section of the hen oviduct during the passage of the egg.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"In chickens, it is formed from the layers of secretions of the anterior section of the hen [oviduct](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oviduct \"Oviduct\") during the passage of the egg.[\\[27\\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eggs_as_food#cite_note-27)","entailment":"The quoted passage explains the origin of egg white in chickens, matching the claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"Egg white consists primarily of approximately 90 percent water into which is dissolved 10 percent proteins.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"Egg white consists primarily of approximately 90 percent water into which is dissolved 10 percent proteins (including [albumins](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Albumin \"Albumin\"), [mucoproteins](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mucoprotein \"Mucoprotein\"), and [globulins](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Globulin \"Globulin\")).","entailment":"The sentence provides the composition percentages of egg white, directly supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"Unlike the yolk, which is high in lipids, egg white contains almost no fat and the carbohydrate content is less than one percent.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"Unlike the yolk, which is high in [lipids](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lipid \"Lipid\") (fats), egg white contains almost no fat and the [carbohydrate](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Carbohydrate \"Carbohydrate\") content is less than one percent.","entailment":"The quoted comparison details the low fat and carbohydrate levels in egg white relative to the yolk, supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"definitional"}]}
